title: iberis intermedia proles collina
intro: Iberis intermedia proles collina, also known as the Dwarf Candytuft, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is primarily grown for its ornamental value due to its vibrant purple flowers and compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Iberis intermedia proles collina, often in the form of teas or infusions, may be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and respiratory infections.
